suggestions.title: Office floor overflows at 1280px desktop viewport
suggestions.description: At 1280×800, the right edge of the office floor is visually clipped — gray overflow shadow bleeds outside the floor container and decorative elements (VACANT desk ad, orange couch prop, plants) are partially outside the viewport. The floor's `max-width: 900px` plus `.main` padding and the `office-wrap` padding adds up to overflow. Fix: reduce floor `max-width` to `860px`, or add `overflow: hidden` to `.office-wrap`, or tighten the horizontal padding on `.main` at this breakpoint. Also audit right-side absolute-positioned children in Office.jsx to ensure they stay within bounds.

suggestions.title: Dashboard tab nearly inaccessible on mobile (375px)
suggestions.description: On a 375px viewport, the '📊 Dashboard' tab button is partially cut off — roughly half the label is invisible. The `.tab-toggle` container has no `overflow-x: auto` and no text truncation strategy for small screens. Users on iPhone SE and similar devices may not realize there's a second tab at all. Fix: add `overflow-x: auto; scrollbar-width: none;` to `.tab-toggle`, or reduce tab padding at ≤480px via a media query (e.g., `padding: 7px 12px; font-size: 0.78rem`). Also consider making tab labels shorter on mobile: 'Office' / 'Dashboard' could compress to icons + short labels.

suggestions.title: App.jsx is a 1350-line god component — extract custom hooks
suggestions.description: App.jsx contains fetching, polling, worker diffing, confetti logic, vibe streak tracking, favicon updates, tab title cycling, toast management, keyboard shortcuts, and the full render tree — all in a single function component. This makes the file extremely hard to test, maintain, or onboard new contributors to. Recommended extractions: `useWorkerSync(pollIntervalActive, pollIntervalHidden)` → returns workers, activityLog, fetchError, isSyncing, syncNow; `useVibeTracking(teamVibeKey)` → returns vibeStreak, vibeHistory, confettiActive; `useTabTitle(activeWorkers, teamVibe)` → side effect only; `useDynamicFavicon(activeWorkers, fetchError)` → side effect only. Office.jsx at 1552 lines has the same problem — Character and CharacterAvatar alone could be a separate file.

suggestions.title: role='radio' on <button> elements is invalid ARIA
suggestions.description: The activity log filter bar and worker filter bar use `role='radio'` on `<button>` elements with a parent `role='radiogroup'`. The ARIA spec prohibits `role='radio'` on `<button>` — it is only valid on `<input type='radio'>` or a generic container. Screen readers like VoiceOver and NVDA will behave unpredictably: some may announce these as 'radio button' but ignore the `aria-checked` state entirely. Fix option A: Replace with actual `<input type='radio'>` elements styled as buttons. Fix option B: Remove `role='radio'` and `aria-checked`, change parent to `role='toolbar'` or `role='group'`, and communicate selection state via `aria-pressed` on each button. Option B is less markup change.

suggestions.title: useTimeTick re-renders entire App every 60 seconds
suggestions.description: `useTimeTick(60_000)` is called at the top of `App` and calls `setTick` every 60 seconds, which triggers a full re-render of the entire component tree including the office floor, stats bar, all worker cards, and activity log — even when no data has changed. The intent is just to refresh relative timestamps like '5m ago'. Fix: Move `useTimeTick` into `ActivityLogEntry` and `WorkerCard` components only, so only those leaf nodes re-render on tick. Alternatively, use a context or a dedicated timestamp refresh event so the tick is scoped. This saves potentially dozens of wasted re-renders per hour.

suggestions.title: completionToast state has a polymorphic shape — runtime type hazard
suggestions.description: The `completionToast` state is used in two incompatible shapes: `{ name: string, color: string }` when a worker ships a task, and `{ text: string, type: 'info' }` when the vibe streak dies. The render uses `completionToast.text ? completionToast.text : '🎉 ${completionToast.name} shipped it!'` to distinguish them, which will silently render '🎉 undefined shipped it!' if the wrong shape is set. Normalize to a single shape: `{ message: string, color?: string }`. Update both call sites to set `message` directly.

suggestions.title: No stale-data fallback — offline load shows empty state
suggestions.description: If a user loads the app with no network connection (or GitHub raw CDN is unreachable), `isLoading` stays true briefly then resolves to an empty workers array with a fetch error banner. The user sees an empty office and no worker cards — there is no cached fallback. Fix: After every successful fetch, persist the workers and activity arrays to `localStorage` (e.g., `bloberto-workers-cache` and `bloberto-activity-cache`). On load, hydrate state from cache before the first fetch resolves, and display a 'Showing last known data' banner if the fetch fails. Workers are small JSON so storage cost is negligible.

suggestions.title: Tab keyboard switch does not manage focus to new panel
suggestions.description: When the user presses '1' or '2' to switch tabs via keyboard shortcuts, focus stays on whatever element was last active (often a button deep in the previous panel). The ARIA authoring guide for tabpanels recommends moving focus to the panel or its first focusable child after a keyboard-triggered tab switch. Currently there is no `focus()` call after `setTab()` in the keyboard handler. Fix: after `setTab('dashboard')`, call `document.getElementById('tabpanel-dashboard')?.focus()` (ensuring the panel has `tabIndex={-1}`). This also applies to the arrow-key navigation in `handleTabKeyDown`.

suggestions.title: Activity log shows blank when filter has no matches — needs empty state
suggestions.description: When filtering the activity log by 'Errors' (or any type/worker combo) with no matching entries, the log renders an empty `<div class='activity-log'>` with no message. The existing `activity-empty` style ('No activity yet…') is only shown when `displayed.length === 0` before filtering — it is never reached when a filter yields zero results because the filter happens at the call site, not inside `ActivityLog`. Fix: pass the active filter label into `ActivityLog` and render a contextual empty state like '🎉 No errors in the last 20 events' or '😶 No activity from Maya recently' when the filtered list is empty.

suggestions.title: App-level constants scattered — move VIBE_COLORS, STAT_DISPATCHES etc. to constants.js
suggestions.description: Several constant objects are defined at the top of App.jsx that logically belong in `utils/constants.js`: `VIBE_COLORS`, `VIBE_RANK`, `STAT_DISPATCHES`, `FOOTER_TAGLINES`, `BONUS_TAGLINES`, `HONORIFICS`, `WORKER_TAGLINES`, `SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES`. Having them in App.jsx bloats the component file and makes them unreachable for unit testing without importing the entire React component tree. Move them to `constants.js` (or a new `utils/copy.js` for the text-heavy ones). This is a pure refactor with no behavior change.

suggestions.title: Worker cards in Dashboard have no visible 'flip to history' affordance on mobile
suggestions.description: The ℹ️ button on each WorkerCard triggers a CSS 3D flip to reveal activity history. On desktop, the button is clearly visible. On mobile, the card is smaller and the button is 22×22px — very close to the status badge, making it easy to miss or accidentally tap. There is also no visual hint that the card is flippable (no 'flip' icon, no tooltip on mobile). Fix: add a subtle 'History' text label next  on mobile, or increase the tap target to 44×44px minimum (WCAG 2.5.5). Also add `title='Tap for activity history'` on the button.

suggestions.title: Character component defines ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES and WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES inside the render function body
suggestions.description: Inside `Character`, `ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES` and `WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES` arrays/objects are declared inside `useEffect` callback scope, meaning new array references are created on every render. While the values are constant, they're not eligible for hoisting by the JS engine because they're declared inside a closure. Move both to module scope (top of Office.jsx, outside any component) so they're created once at module load time. Same applies to `IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS`.

suggestions.title: Light mode office floor grid is nearly invisible
suggestions.description: In `[data-theme='light']`, the floor grid uses `--grid-line: rgba(0,0,0,0.05)` — this is so faint that the grid character of the office floor essentially disappears in light mode, making it look like a plain light rectangle instead of a grid office. The dark mode uses `rgba(255,255,255,0.04)` which is comparable but the floor is very dark so the grid reads. Fix: increase light mode grid opacity to `rgba(0,0,0,0.09)` or `rgba(100,100,180,0.08)` for a subtle blue-tinted grid that matches the office aesthetic.

suggestions.title: Footer quote pagination counter '1/9' has no label and is cryptically small
suggestions.description: The footer renders a tiny `1/9`-style counter next to the rotating tagline quote, at `font-size: 0.7em` with `opacity: 0.55`. Users don't know what this number means — is it a version? a step? a rating? Fix: add a `title='Quote 1 of 9 — click ✦ to cycle'` tooltip on the counter span, or replace the bare `1/9` with `Quote 1/9` at slightly higher opacity. Also consider making the ✦ sparkle icon visually larger and adding a `cursor: pointer` hint text on hover.

suggestions.title: Office tab has no worker presence indicator — must switch tabs to check
suggestions.description: The Dashboard tab correctly shows an error badge count (red pill with number) when workers are in error state. But the Office tab has zero status indicators — if you're on the Dashboard tab, you have no idea if the office view has something interesting happening (new arrival, door event, worker just errored). Add a small presence dot or count indicator to the Office tab: a green dot when everyone is working, amber when someone is idle, red when someone is in error. This mirrors how the tab error badge works for Dashboard.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline hover legend only shows on hover — inaccessible on touch devices
suggestions.description: The `.sparkline-legend` in the Team Vibe stat card is hidden by default and only revealed on `.stat-card:hover`. Touch users (mobile, tablet) cannot hover, so they never see the legend showing which vibe each colored dot represents. The SVG `<title>` elements provide screen-reader text but nothing visual on mobile. Fix: always render the legend (remove the hover-only display rule) but keep it small and subtle. The pills are tiny (`0.55rem`) so they won't add much visual weight.

suggestions.title: Inline styles in completionToast and section headers bypass the theme system
suggestions.description: The `completionToast` element in App.jsx uses a long `style={{...}}` object with hardcoded values for `position`, `background`, `border`, `borderRadius`, `padding`, `fontSize`, `fontWeight`, `color`, `boxShadow`, `zIndex`, `whiteSpace`, `pointerEvents`, and `animation`. Section header subtitles also use inline `style={{ fontSize: '0.8rem', color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}`. These are hard to override in a theme, hard to find when debugging, and add noise to JSX. Extract to CSS classes: `.completion-toast` and `.section-subtitle`. This is cosmetic but improves maintainability.

suggestions.title: Add task search / filter-by-text to the Dashboard worker grid
suggestions.description: As the team grows (data could have 10+ workers), there's no way to search worker cards by name or task text. The tag filter (Bug/Ship/Design/QA/Data/Docs) is great but only works on tasks that match the defined regex patterns. A simple text input above the workers grid that filters cards by worker name or task string would cover the gap. Keep it minimal: a small `<input placeholder='Search workers…'>` that dims non-matching cards using the existing `worker-card--dimmed` class.

suggestions.title: Polling-paused banner overlaps header controls on small screens
suggestions.description: The `.polling-paused-pill` renders inline inside `.header-right` with `flex-wrap: wrap`. On viewports below ~500px, this pill wraps to a new line and sits awkwardly inside the sticky header, pushing content down and occasionally obscuring the tab bar. The text 'Paused · move mouse to refresh' is also not meaningful on touch devices (no mouse). Fix: hide this pill on `(pointer: coarse)` devices (same pattern as shortcut-toast), or reword to 'Tap to refresh' and use a fixed bottom banner instead of injecting it into the header.
